title: Entryway Accessory Wall Cubby Organizer
summary: A wall-mounted grid of 5" x 10" cubbies for hats, gloves, and small accessories — a nested inner grid built separately, then dropped into an outer frame.
description:
This is genuinely a build-to-fit design: the source measures your specific wall space first and generates a custom cut list from that, rather than shipping one fixed size. The dimensions below are one reasonable reference build (a 30" x 40" organizer with a 2-across, 3-high grid of roughly 5" x 10" cubbies) — measure your own space and scale the shelf and divider count up or down using the same construction method.

The build order is what makes this manageable: assemble the outer frame first, then build the inner grid of dividers and shelves as its own separate unit, and drop that finished grid into the frame rather than fighting to fit each divider individually in place. Cut a couple of blocks to your shelf height and keep them on hand as spacers — they're faster and more consistent than measuring and marking every divider location by hand.

materials.name: Pine, 1x6, assorted lengths
materials.note: Cross-cut to length from 1x6 stock for every part — sides, top, bottom, dividers, and shelves.

materials.name: Wall mounting screws
materials.note: Length depends on your attachment method — studs, cleat, or adjacent cabinet.

steps.title: Plan and measure your space
steps.body: Measure the wall space where the organizer will hang and decide on overall dimensions before cutting anything. As a starting point, cubbies of roughly 5" x 10" work well for hats and gloves — plan for a top and bottom piece, with no gap needed below the bottom shelf. Depth isn't critical, since every part is cross-cut from the same 1x6 stock.

steps.title: Build the outer frame
steps.body: Assemble the sides, top, and bottom with 1-1/4" brad nails and wood glue to form the outer frame.

steps.title: Build the inner grid
steps.body: Build the inner grid of dividers and shelves as its own separate assembly, then place it inside the outer frame and attach. Some corners will need nailing at an angle to reach. Use cut-to-height spacer blocks to help place both the vertical dividers and the horizontal shelves consistently, rather than measuring and marking every location individually.

steps.title: Place the remaining shelves
steps.body: Continue placing any remaining shelves, again using a cut-to-height spacer block as your guide.

steps.title: Attach the back and hang
steps.body:
Mark the location of every center shelf and divider on the outside of the sides. Cut the 1/4" plywood back to size, run glue along the back edges of the shelves and dividers, and set the plywood in place. Use a straight edge to connect your marks and guide nail placement, then attach the back with 5/8" brad nails and glue.

Hang through the back plywood into wall studs, or use a picture-hanging kit or French cleat mounted to the back if studs aren't available where you need them.

images.alt: Entryway wall-mounted cubby organizer for hats and gloves
